articles.headline: Coalition Urges OpenAI to Scrap AI Ballot Measure Over Child Safety Concerns
articles.body_snippet: Advocacy groups say the OpenAI-backed measure would limit legal accountability and lock in narrow protections for children.

articles.headline: Algorand Foundation Cuts 25% of Staff as Crypto Industry Layoffs Grow
articles.body_snippet: The organization behind layer-1 blockchain Algorand laid off 25% of its staff due to macroeconomic uncertainty and lower crypto prices.

articles.headline: Trump White House Registers Aliens.gov—Is the UFO File Drop Imminent?
articles.body_snippet: The White House registered aliens.gov, a month after Trump ordered the release of all government UAP and UFO files following a viral Obama moment.

articles.headline: From Coinbase to Ripple: The Biggest Crypto Cases Dumped by Trump's SEC
articles.body_snippet: Under President Trump, the SEC has backed down from nearly all its fights with crypto firms. These are the biggest cases ended so far.

articles.headline: Bitcoin, Ethereum Waver as Fed Holds Interest Rates Steady
articles.body_snippet: Inflation has exceeded the central bank’s 2% target for nearly five years, but the Fed held rates firm again as Bitcoin and Ethereum wobbled.

articles.headline: Myriad Traders Slash Spring Rally Chances as Bitcoin, Ethereum Slide
articles.body_snippet: A hotter-than-expected inflation reading pushed crypto prices lower Wednesday—slashing the chances of a broad spring breakout.

articles.headline: You Can Now Trade Official S&P 500 Perpetual Futures via Hyperliquid
articles.body_snippet: The S&P 500 index was licensed to Trade[XYZ], a provider of markets for real-world assets on Hyperliquid for perpetual futures.

articles.headline: Crypto Market Structure Bill to Face Key Vote in April and Must Pass by May, Senators Say
articles.body_snippet: Sen. Bernie Moreno said if the Clarity Act isn’t passed by May, “digital asset legislation will not pass for the foreseeable future.”

articles.headline: Pudgy Penguins Launched A New Game. Crypto Scammers Made A Fake Version
articles.body_snippet: Malwarebytes has warned that a phishing campaign is mimicking the Pudgy World game to steal user credentials

articles.headline: Stripe-Backed Tempo Network Launches With Focus on AI Agent Payments
articles.body_snippet: Tempo, the layer-1 blockchain backed by payments giant Stripe, launched its mainnet with a clear focus on AI agent payments.

articles.headline: Citi Downgrades Crypto Exchange Gemini After Cutting Bitcoin, Ethereum Price Targets
articles.body_snippet: Gemini stock (GEMI) fell 16% on Wednesday following the downgrade and a broader market dip, after Citi cut its Bitcoin and Ethereum targets.

articles.headline: 'Crypto Castle': YouTube Comedy Takes You Back to When Bitcoin Was Just $250
articles.body_snippet: “The Crypto Castle” takes a look at the early days of Bitcoin—and the "sad evolution" it's undergone since then.

articles.headline: Bitcoin, Ethereum Slip on Inflation Surprise as Oil Prices Jump
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin fell alongside U.S. stocks after the world's largest gas field came under attack amid hotter-than-expected inflation data.

articles.headline: Canadian Regulator Revokes Registrations of 23 Crypto Firms
articles.body_snippet: Canada's FINTRAC pulled the licenses of multiple money services businesses, in a "significantly increased pace of action."

articles.headline: Bitcoin ETFs' $1.2B Streak Hangs in Balance as FOMC Takes Center Stage
articles.body_snippet: Experts warn that Bitcoin ETF inflows remain "episodic" without policy shifts, ahead of today's FOMC meeting.

articles.headline: Morning Minute: The SEC & CFTC Declare 'Most Crypto Assets' Are Not Securities
articles.body_snippet: After a decade of lawsuits, the SEC and CFTC finally wrote the rules for digital assets.

articles.headline: Forget AGI—Top AI Models Still Struggle With Math
articles.body_snippet: New benchmark study results show leading AI models still lag humans in visual math reasoning.

articles.headline: UK Panel Calls Crypto Donations 'High Risk,' Seeks Immediate Ban
articles.body_snippet: A cross-party committee has warned that cryptocurrency donations leave UK politics open to foreign interference.

articles.headline: Bitcoin Depot Flags Control 'Weaknesses' as Connecticut Halts Its Operations
articles.body_snippet: Its license was suspended after regulators accused the Bitcoin ATM operator of overcharging kiosk users and failing to provide some refunds.

articles.headline: Democrats Press Meta Over Facial Recognition Plans for Smart Glasses
articles.body_snippet: Lawmakers are citing privacy risks tied to wearable AI and are asking how Meta intends to secure consent from both users and bystanders.

articles.headline: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 Launch Sparks Meme Frenzy as Gamers Balk at AI ‘Neural Rendering’
articles.body_snippet: NVIDIA pitched DLSS 5 as a breakthrough in real-time rendering. Players saw something closer to AI overreach.

articles.headline: Crypto Gift Card Platform Bitrefill Discloses Hack, Points Finger at North Korean Groups
articles.body_snippet: Bitrefill, which lets users swap Bitcoin, Dogecoin and other crypto assets for gift cards, disclosed a breach that took place on March 1.

articles.headline: Crypto Bill Stablecoin Yield Compromise Could Come This Week: Tim Scott
articles.body_snippet: The White House is prepared to announce progress as soon as tomorrow on the issue plaguing crypto's market structure bill.

articles.headline: Arizona Files Charges Against Kalshi, Calling Prediction Market an 'Illegal Gambling Operation'
articles.body_snippet: Prediction market platform Kalshi was hit with 20 criminal charges in Arizona, which alleged that it's an "illegal gambling operation."

articles.headline: CFTC Clears Phantom to Connect Users to Regulated Derivatives Markets
articles.body_snippet: Under the ruling, the developer of the Phantom self-custody crypto wallet avoids having to register as a broker.

articles.headline: SEC Declares 'Most Crypto Assets' Not Securities, Including Staking, Airdrops and Bitcoin Mining
articles.body_snippet: SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the new securities guidance, which impacts "most crypto assets," provides "clear lines in clear terms."

articles.headline: What If Elon Musk's Grok Leaks Classified Info? Elizabeth Warren Is Worried—The Pentagon Isn't
articles.body_snippet: Sen. Warren is demanding answers after the Pentagon handed Elon Musk's xAI classified network access—despite NSA warnings and a trail of harmful AI outputs.

articles.headline: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 Mini and Nano, Which Could Be More Useful Than the Big Model
articles.body_snippet: OpenAI's new small models are faster and cheaper than GPT-5.4, and for most everyday use cases, that's exactly what developers and businesses actually need.

articles.headline: Urea Surges 34% as Iran Conflict Ripples Through Commodities, Bitcoin
articles.body_snippet: The Iran conflict is impacting on commodities beyond oil, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz chokes off shipping.

articles.headline: PayPal Expands PYUSD Stablecoin Globally as Supply Tops $4 Billion
articles.body_snippet: PayPal’s PYUSD is expanding to a total of 70 international markets as the payments giant’s stablecoin tops a $4 billion market cap.

articles.headline: Democrats Target Prediction Market Insider Trading, Claim Trump Allies Are Profiting
articles.body_snippet: Democratic lawmakers introduced legislation prohibiting prediction markets that hinge on government action or pre-determined outcomes.

articles.headline: Allium Brings 65TB of Data from Bitcoin, Ethereum, Sui and More to Walrus
articles.body_snippet: The partnership aims to lay the foundations for the agentic workflows that will become increasingly important to on-chain finance.

articles.headline: XRP Flips BNB, Hitting Highest Price in a Month as Ripple Plans to Seek Brazil License
articles.body_snippet: The Ripple-linked XRP has leapfrogged BNB in market cap as the crypto payments firm expands its Brazil push.

articles.headline: Sam Altman’s World Taps Coinbase’s Open Protocol to Verify Humans Behind AI Agents
articles.body_snippet: A new toolkit aims to allow AI agents to prove that a real person stands behind them when interacting online.

articles.headline: Morning Minute: Strategy's $1.58B Buy Pushes Bitcoin to $75k
articles.body_snippet: Strategy just made its biggest Bitcoin buy of 2026, and Kalshi is running a $1 billion March Madness bracket contest.

articles.headline: Algorand Foundation cuts 25% of staff, citing macro uncertainty
articles.body_snippet: The Algorand Foundation said it has a “more sustainable alignment” of resources with the protocol’s long-term business priorities.

articles.headline: Fold Q4 revenue up, CEO sees Bitcoin rewards overtaking air miles
articles.body_snippet: Fold CEO Will Reeves said it is focused on scaling its 2026 product line after paying off two convertible debts, removing overhang and enabling it to focus on growth.

articles.headline: Bitcoin falls under $71K but data shows BTC’s bullish momentum holding
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin dropped below $71,000, but the market’s tilt toward bulls holds as spot ETF inflows and BTC buying from Strategy boost investor sentiment.

articles.headline: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.body_snippet: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.headline: Fed leaves rates unchanged, says geopolitical uncertainty clouds outlook
articles.body_snippet: The effect on energy prices from the Iran war will impact the economy, but the size and scope of the macroeconomic shock are still unknown.

articles.headline: Kalshi CEO fires back against Arizona criminal charges as ‘total overstep‘
articles.body_snippet: The prediction markets co-founder said that the company would “abide by court decisions“ but signaled that the charges were based partly on political bias and media attention.

articles.headline: SEC Chair explains why NFTs fall outside of securities laws
articles.body_snippet: Paul Atkins says nonfungible tokens are typically collectibles, not investment contracts, as the agency outlines new categories of digital assets outside securities laws.

articles.headline: FTX Recovery Trust to distribute $2.2B to creditors in March
articles.body_snippet: The fourth round of reimbursements to creditors and former clients of the failed crypto exchange since February 2025 brings the total paid to about $10 billion.

articles.headline: Crypto Fear and Greed rebounds off extreme lows as traders re-enter
articles.body_snippet: The Crypto Fear and Greed Index just ended a 48-day stretch in the “extreme fear” zone, signalling improving sentiment among investors. Will fresh capital inflows reignite the bull market?

articles.headline: ‘We are so close this time‘ — Senator Lummis on market structure bill
articles.body_snippet: The Wyoming Republican said that the main issue holding up passage of the bill was stablecoin yield, while adding that she believed a provision on DeFi had been ”put to bed.”

articles.headline: Fed holds rates amid higher inflation outlook: Bitcoin bounces to $72K
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin’s pre-FOMC sell-off eased as the US Federal Reserve's choice to leave interest rates unchanged was followed by a swift bounce in BTC price.

articles.headline: S&P Dow Jones licenses S&P 500 perpetual futures for Hyperliquid
articles.body_snippet: The new derivative allows eligible non-US users to trade leveraged exposure to the index around the clock using official data on a decentralized platform.

articles.headline: ‘Rich Dad, Poor Dad’ author says ‘pin is near’ on TradFi ‘bubble burst:’ Predicts $750K Bitcoin
articles.body_snippet: Author and personal finance educator Robert Kiyosaki says Bitcoin is going to $750,000, but there's a catch.

articles.headline: Bhutan offloads an additional $72.3M Bitcoin amid market downturn
articles.body_snippet: After the Kingdom of Bhutan's BTC reserve peaked in October 2024, when it held more than 13,000 coins, it has been pared to just 4,400 Bitcoin.

articles.headline: Bitcoin tests fresh decoupling trade as tech correlation drops to 2018 lows
articles.body_snippet: BTC price is vastly outperforming the tech-heavy Nasdaq index amid the US–Iran war, but its risks of crashing toward $51,000 persist.

articles.headline: Japan’s SBI VC Trade launches retail USDC lending as stablecoin use grows
articles.body_snippet: SBI VC Trade said users could lend assets directly to its platform, but added that the company may re-lend funds as part of its operations.

articles.headline: Crypto industry ties were a liability in Illinois primary
articles.body_snippet: Ties to the crypto industry became a vector for attack in a recent Democratic primary election in the state of Illinois.

articles.headline: Circle’s policy chief tells UK to merge MiCA clarity with US stablecoin rules
articles.body_snippet: The UK has a unique opportunity to merge the best of the EU’s MiCA framework and the US GENIUS Act, Circle’s Dante Disparte told the House of Lords committee on Wednesday.

articles.headline: Bitcoin dips to $72K on hot US PPI inflation hours before Fed FOMC meeting
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin price action fell to week-to-date lows with Bitcoin caught between high US inflation and nerves over Fed policy hints at the day's FOMC meeting.

articles.headline: 74% of institutions expect crypto prices to rise in 12 months: Survey
articles.body_snippet: A Coinbase-EY study reveals institutional investors plan to boost crypto allocations in 2026, favoring regulated products as stablecoins and tokenization gain traction.

articles.headline: Crypto firms’ open letter urges top US colleges to teach DeFi
articles.body_snippet: Crypto firms argue that DeFi should be taught at top schools so that students can eventually take on a rapid rise in non-technical crypto jobs on Wall Street.

articles.headline: Crypto cards aren't the future, but onchain credit is
articles.body_snippet: Crypto cards force asset sales and tax hits. Onchain credit enables yield-bearing collateral power spending without liquidation, making cards obsolete interfaces.

articles.headline: RedotPay defends team reshuffle amid funding talks and IPO plans
articles.body_snippet: RedotPay says it restructured teams for efficiency after a Bloomberg report flagged executive turnover, China sensitivities and new funding talks.

articles.headline: Connecticut suspends Bitcoin Depot as 2026 revenue outlook worsens
articles.body_snippet: Connecticut halts Bitcoin Depot’s operations, with the company expecting lower revenue in 2026 amid mounting regulatory pressure and a steep decline in its stock price.

articles.headline: UK lawmakers seek moratorium on crypto donations to political parties
articles.body_snippet: A UK parliamentary committee urged an immediate moratorium on crypto political donations until stronger safeguards are in place.

articles.headline: Tally winds down, citing lack of viable market for DAO tooling
articles.body_snippet: Tally said its platform served more than one million users, supporting governance across hundreds of organizations and processing over $1 billion in payments.

articles.headline: Bitcoin stalls at $76K: Key BTC price levels to watch ahead of FOMC
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin price traded at $74,000 as investors braced for Jerome Powell’s post-FOMC speech that could see volatile swings toward key BTC price levels.

articles.headline: Ethereum aims to cut bridge times by 98% to 13 seconds with new rule
articles.body_snippet: Ethereum’s FCR aims to reduce bridge times by up to 98%, bringing L1-to-L2 and exchange deposits down to 13 seconds without a hard fork.

articles.headline: Bitcoin ETF inflow streak nears October run, but totals still lag
articles.body_snippet: US spot Bitcoin ETFs draw $1.2 billion over seven days, far short of October 2025’s nine-day $6 billion streak, as XRP ETFs turn green.

articles.headline: Australian crypto shopping surges, but so do banking blocks: Survey
articles.body_snippet: Online shopping was the leading real-world use case for crypto among Australians, followed by paying for services such as freelancing and video game purchases.
